description: Replacement profile guard definition. In replacement mode (L2/L3), no symbol may call through to host glibc. In interpose mode (L0/L1), call-throughs are allowed for symbols on the interpose_allowlist. This file is the source of truth for the CI gate scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh.

interpose.description: LD_PRELOAD interpose mode. Call-through to host glibc is permitted for allowlisted modules.

replacement.description: Full glibc replacement mode. Zero call-through permitted. Every symbol must be Implemented or RawSyscall.

detection_rules.description: A call-through is any libc:: function call that is NOT a raw syscall, constant, or type reference.

interpose_allowlist.description: Modules permitted to call through to host glibc in interpose (LD_PRELOAD) mode when the source scan detects direct escape hatches.
interpose_allowlist.rationale: Interpose mode permits direct host escape hatches in these modules when they are present; replacement mode forbids them.

dlfcn_boundary_policy.description: dlfcn-specific boundary contract between interpose and replacement profiles.

replacement_forbidden.description: In replacement mode, these call-through patterns must be zero. The gate scans for libc:: function calls (not syscall/constant/type) and host pthread wrapper calls (host_pthread_*) and fails if any are found outside the allowlist. Additionally, pthread_mutex_* call-through is always forbidden in replacement-relevant routing paths.

call_through_census.description: Current census of direct host libc/host pthread escape hatches by ABI module, as scanned by scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh.

dlfcn_abi.note: dlfcn phase-1 boundary remains native and must not emit direct host dl* call-through tokens.

dirent_abi.note: Directory-entry allocation helpers intentionally pair host malloc/free in interpose mode.

glibc_internal_abi.note: Internal compatibility helpers allocate host-visible directory/scratch buffers and preserve low-level diagnostic abort/write fallbacks in interpose mode.

io_internal_abi.note: Temporary-file probing remains host-backed in interpose mode.

pthread_abi.note: pthread_sigqueue is resolved through the host pthread wrapper in interpose mode only.

rpc_abi.note: RPC/XDR compatibility helpers still allocate host-visible buffers in interpose mode.

signal_abi.note: Signal compatibility helpers still delegate selected sigset/action paths to host libc in interpose mode.

stdio_abi.note: Stdio compatibility helpers retain selected host environment/string probes in interpose mode.

stdlib_abi.note: Selected stdlib helpers still use host-visible allocation and descriptor probing in interpose mode.

string_abi.note: strdup/strndup and GNU argz APIs use host malloc/free so caller-owned buffers pair with libc::free in interpose and test builds.

unistd_abi.note: Unistd helpers retain host-visible allocation and selected legacy diagnostic/time probes in interpose mode.

wchar_abi.note: Wide-string duplication returns a host-freeable allocation in interpose mode.

call_through_census.note: Census tracks direct host call-through scan patterns used by scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh.
